secretPrompt.privacyNote: This goes straight to your encrypted vault. The AI never sees it.
secretPrompt.revealWarning: The agent asks to SEE this secret’s raw value. If you approve, the value is sent to the AI model for one turn, then automatically removed from the conversation history. Approve only if you understand why it needs it.
secretPrompt.oauthHint: Sign in to {{provider}} in the browser tab that opens, then paste the authorization code it gives you below.
secretPrompt.oauthLoopbackHint: After approving, the browser shows a localhost error. That's expected: copy the whole address from the address bar and paste it below.
secretPrompt.oauthLoopbackPlaceholder: Paste the localhost URL (or the code)

add_llm_provider.title: Add an LLM provider
add_llm_provider.description: Required to chat with Agents. Without it the chat engine has nothing to call. Add a provider like Anthropic, OpenAI, or any plugin (Mistral, etc.).

set_default_llm.title: Set a default LLM
set_default_llm.description: Used as the fallback model for new Agents and for the Agent Wizard's AI-assisted creation. Without it, you'll have to pick a model manually each time.

add_embedding_provider.title: Add an embedding provider
add_embedding_provider.description: Unlocks semantic memory search. Without it, memory falls back to keyword-only (FTS): still functional but less accurate at finding related memories.

add_image_provider.title: Add an image provider
add_image_provider.description: Unlocks AI avatar generation for Agents and the `generate_image` tool. Without it, Agents use initials avatars and can't generate images.

add_search_provider.title: Add a search provider
add_search_provider.description: Unlocks the `web_search` tool so Agents can fetch live information. Without it, web_search returns an explicit error message.

add_voice_provider.title: Add voice providers (TTS / STT)
add_voice_provider.description: Unlocks `text_to_speech` and `transcribe_audio` tools, plus voice messages on channels that support them (e.g. WhatsApp voice notes).

create_first_agent.title: Create your first Agent
create_first_agent.description: Agents are the AI agents you actually talk to. The wizard uses your default LLM to suggest a personality from a short description, or you can configure everything manually.

unsavedChanges.title: Unsaved changes
unsavedChanges.description: You have unsaved changes that will be lost if you close this dialog. Are you sure you want to discard them?

errors.backendUnavailable: Unable to reach the server. Please check that the backend is running and try again.
errors.agentErrorGeneric: An error occurred while processing your request

login.title: Welcome back
login.subtitle: Sign in to your Gezy workspace
login.forgotPassword: Forgot your password? Contact your workspace admin.

connectAi.why: This provider powers your Agents and Queenie, your setup guide. Pick one built-in provider to get started. You can add more later, including plugins for other providers.

bootstrap.title: Connect your AI
bootstrap.description: To bring your assistant to life, connect one AI provider. Pick a built-in one to start. You can add more later, including plugins for other providers.
bootstrap.noLlm: No LLM provider found. Please connect one.

modal.stopBody: You can pick up any time by chatting with Queenie in your Agent list. Your conversation is saved.

rescue.title: Something went wrong
rescue.body: Queenie hit an error: {{error}}. This is often a provider key or configuration problem. Let Queenie help you fix it.
rescue.message: Something just failed with an error. It looks like a provider key or configuration problem. Can you help me diagnose and reconfigure the provider?

identity.title: Your identity
identity.subtitle: Tell us about yourself
identity.avatarHint: Click to upload or drag & drop
identity.pseudonymHint: How your Agents will address you

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.subtitle: Customize your experience
preferences.languageHint: Language of menus, buttons, and pages.
preferences.agentLanguageHint: Language your Agents will use when talking to you. Agents can speak nearly any language, even ones the interface is not translated into.
preferences.themeHint: You can change this anytime in settings
preferences.contrastHint: Soft slightly reduces contrast for a gentler look.

providers.familiesHint: A single provider row is created with the selected capabilities. The same API key powers every family you enable. Toggling them later is a row edit, not a new entry.
providers.name: Display name
providers.nameTip: A friendly label to identify this provider in your dashboard. If left empty, the provider type name is used.
providers.signinPanelHint: A browser tab will open to sign in to {{provider}}. After you approve, the page shows an authorization code. Paste it below to connect.
providers.signinLoopbackHint: After you approve, the browser tries to open a localhost address and shows an error. That's expected: copy the full address from the address bar and paste it below.
providers.signinLoopbackPlaceholder: Paste the localhost URL (or the code)

agents.title: Agents
agents.emptyDescription: Create your first AI assistant to get started

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.emptyDescription: Tasks will appear here when Agents are working

queueIndicator.aria: Task queue: {{running}} of {{slots}} slots busy, {{queued}} queued

crons.title: Scheduled Jobs
crons.emptyDescription: Schedule recurring tasks for your Agents

miniApps.title: Mini-Apps
miniApps.emptyDescription: Ask an Agent to create one
miniApps.noAgentSelectedDescription: Select an Agent to see its mini-apps

edit.title: Edit Agent
edit.slugHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Used in URLs and inter-Agent communication.

create.title: Create a new Agent
create.name: Name
create.providerHint: Multiple providers can serve this model. Choose which one this Agent should use, or leave on Auto to use the first available.
create.noLlmHint: The Model picker below will be empty until an LLM provider is added. Save is disabled without a model.
create.characterHint: Define personality, tone, and communication style. This shapes how the Agent speaks and behaves in conversations.
create.expertiseHint: Describe what the Agent knows and what it should focus on. This guides its responses and areas of competence.
create.memoryEmptyDescription: Once your Agent is created, it will automatically build memories from conversations. You can also add memories manually from the edit dialog.
create.compactionEmptyDescription: Once your Agent is created, you can override global compaction settings here.
create.thinkingEmptyDescription: Once your Agent is created, you can enable extended thinking for compatible models.
create.nameHint: A short, memorable name for this Agent.
create.roleHint: A brief description of what this Agent does (shown in the sidebar).
create.nameTip: A short, recognizable name. This is how the Agent appears in the sidebar and in conversations.
create.roleTip: A one-line summary of what this Agent does. Shown under the name in the sidebar.
create.modelTip: The AI model powering this Agent. Different models have different strengths - larger models are smarter but slower and more expensive.
create.scoutModelTip: A cheap, fast model used by the `scout` tool when this Agent (or its tasks) delegates heavy read-only exploration. A project's scout default takes precedence over this setting. Leave on inherit to fall back to the global scout default, then the Agent's own model.
create.scoutModelHint: Used by the `scout` tool to delegate large read-only exploration (locating code, mapping a subsystem) to a cheaper model. Priority: per-call override → project scout default → this setting → global default → this Agent's model.
create.characterTip: Defines the Agent's personality: how it talks, its tone, quirks, and communication style. Think of it as the Agent's "soul".
create.expertiseTip: Describes what the Agent knows and should focus on. This guides its answers, priorities, and areas of competence.
create.scoutThinkingTip: Reasoning effort for this Agent's scouts. A project-level scout reasoning takes precedence; per-call overrides beat everything. Inherit = fall back to the global default, then this Agent's own config.
create.soulEmptyDescription: Create the Agent first, then customize its SOUL/persona from this tab.

settings.title: Agent settings
settings.dangerZoneDesc: Permanently delete this Agent and all of its data. This cannot be undone.
settings.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? This will permanently delete this Agent and all its data.

tools.saveMcpHint: Save the Agent first to configure MCP tools.
tools.connectionFailed: Connection failed - unable to discover tools from this server.

missingCapability.image: Needs an image provider: toggle works but the tool will return an error until one is configured.
missingCapability.search: Needs a search provider: toggle works but the tool will return an error until one is configured.
missingCapability.tts: Needs a text-to-speech provider: toggle works but the tool will return an error until one is configured.
missingCapability.stt: Needs a speech-to-text provider: toggle works but the tool will return an error until one is configured.
missingCapability.embedding: Needs an embedding provider: memory recall and storage will fail until one is configured.

tools.toolboxesHint: Pick one or more toolboxes to grant this Agent its tools. A toolbox bundles tools across all sources (native, plugin, MCP, custom).
tools.defaultsToAll: No toolbox selected: this Agent gets the built-in 'all' toolbox (every native and custom tool).
tools.noToolboxesDescription: Create toolboxes from Settings to grant tools to this Agent.
tools.resolvedPreviewHint: Read-only preview of the tools the selected toolboxes grant. Listed tools that don't exist are skipped.
tools.resolvedPreviewEmpty: No tools granted by the current selection.

extras.title: Individual grants
extras.hint: Tools granted on top of toolboxes: approved requests land here.
extras.dialogHint: Pick the tools to grant this Agent on top of its toolboxes.

defaults.character:
You are a friendly, thoughtful, and versatile assistant. You communicate clearly and adapt your tone to the context - casual when appropriate, precise when needed.

You are patient, curious, and genuinely enjoy helping. When you don't know something, you say so honestly. You ask clarifying questions when the request is ambiguous.

You keep your responses concise unless the user explicitly asks for detail.
defaults.expertise:
You have broad general knowledge and can help with a wide range of topics:
- Writing, editing, and summarizing text
- Research, analysis, and problem-solving
- Brainstorming and creative tasks
- Explaining complex concepts simply
- Planning and organizing

You adapt your level of detail and technicality based on what the user needs.

compacting.title: Compaction
compacting.overrideHint: Leave empty to use global defaults.
compacting.thresholdPercentHint: Compaction triggers when context usage exceeds this percentage of the model's context window.
compacting.keepPercentHint: Percentage of context window kept as raw messages after compaction.
compacting.summaryBudgetHint: When summaries exceed this % of the context window, oldest ones are merged together.
compacting.maxSummariesHint: Maximum number of active summaries before triggering a merge.
compacting.absoluteCapsHint: Hard limits in real tokens. They bound the footprint on large-window models (e.g. 1M), where the percentages above would otherwise be huge. The effective budget is the smaller of the percentage and the cap.
compacting.keepMaxTokensHint: Maximum raw messages kept after compaction, regardless of window size. Caps the keep percentage.
compacting.triggerMaxTokensHint: Compaction fires once context reaches this many tokens, even if the percentage threshold isn't met. Caps the trigger threshold.
compacting.summaryMaxTokensHint: Summaries are merged once they exceed this many tokens. Caps the summary budget.
compacting.animFilling: Messages fill the context window as the conversation progresses.
compacting.animApproaching: Context is filling up. Compaction will trigger when the threshold is reached.
compacting.animCompacting: Threshold reached! Oldest messages are being compressed into a summary.
compacting.animMerging: Too many summaries: oldest ones are being merged together.
compacting.animDone: Cycle complete. The process repeats indefinitely as the conversation grows.
compacting.animContinuing: Conversation continues. {{summaries}} summary(ies) preserved, new messages keep flowing.
compacting.animExplanation: When the context window fills up, the oldest messages are compressed into dated summaries. These summaries accumulate chronologically. When too many summaries pile up, the oldest ones are merged together, keeping recent summaries detailed and older ones compressed.

thinking.title: Thinking / Reasoning
thinking.description: When enabled, compatible models will reason step-by-step before responding. This can improve quality for complex tasks but uses more tokens. Not all models support this feature.
thinking.effortHint: Levels are limited to what the selected model supports. Higher levels reason longer and use more tokens.

avatar.title: Choose avatar
avatar.manualDescription: Fine-tune this one avatar. Style and type are pre-filled from your global avatar settings; add any extra details below. Nothing here changes the global settings.
avatar.baseImageCustomHint: Your custom base image (used only if the model supports image-to-image).
avatar.baseImageDefaultHint: The default base image (used only if the model supports image-to-image).
avatar.subjectPlaceholder: e.g. a small, friendly, cute robot
avatar.characterPlaceholder: Optional: add details just for this avatar (e.g. round glasses, teal palette, holding a tiny book). Leave empty to derive everything from the Agent's identity.
avatar.autoDescription: The Agent generates its own portrait based on its identity. Its name, role, and personality all influence the result - make sure they're filled in first.
avatar.noImageProvider: No image provider configured. Add one in Settings.
avatar.createFirst: Save the Agent first to enable generation.
avatar.uploadHint: Click to select or drag & drop an image (any size)

wizard.title: Describe your Agent
wizard.subtitle: Describe the assistant you need and we'll configure it for you.
wizard.placeholder: e.g. An expert in corporate law who can help draft contracts and advise on compliance...
wizard.refinePlaceholder: e.g. Make it more formal, add tax law expertise...
wizard.generateError: Failed to generate configuration. Please try again or create manually.
wizard.noLlmHint: AI-assisted setup needs an LLM provider. Add one in Settings → Providers, or click 'Create manually' to fill the form yourself.

agent.modelUnavailableHint: This Agent's model is no longer available. Update the model in Agent settings or add the provider back.
agent.modelUnavailableInput: Cannot send messages - this Agent's model is unavailable
agent.deleteConfirm: This action cannot be undone. All conversations and data associated with this Agent will be permanently deleted.

soul.title: SOUL - Agent Persona
soul.bannerTitle: The SOUL defines who your Agent is
soul.bannerDescription: This is the core personality prompt - the Agent's identity, values, reasoning style, and behavioral guidelines. When empty, a thoughtful default persona is used automatically. Edit this to give your Agent a unique character.
soul.editorHint: Define personality, tone, reasoning style, and behavioral guidelines. This is injected as the core of the system prompt.
soul.defaultPreviewText: You are {{name}}, a thoughtful AI assistant. You think step-by-step and explain your reasoning when the problem warrants it. You are honest about your limitations and ask for clarification when needed. You strive to be helpful while being safe and responsible. When performing tasks, you plan before acting and verify your results.

create.title: Create scheduled job
create.name: Name
create.taskDescriptionPlaceholder: What should the Agent do at each execution...
create.targetAgentHint: If set, this Agent executes the task instead of the owner.
create.modelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the Agent's default
create.nameTip: A descriptive name to identify this job. Shown in the sidebar and notifications.
create.agentTip: The Agent that owns and runs this job. It will execute the task instructions on each trigger.
create.scheduleTip: Standard cron expression (5 fields). Use the presets below or write your own. Times are in the server's timezone.
create.taskDescriptionTip: Detailed instructions for the Agent. Be specific about what it should do, check, or produce at each execution.
create.targetAgentTip: Optionally delegate execution to a different Agent. Useful when the owner Agent dispatches work to a specialist.
create.modelTip: Override the Agent's default model for this job. Useful for running cheaper models on routine tasks.
create.toolboxesHelp: Which tool sets the tasks spawned by this job can use, on top of the mandatory core set. Leave empty for the full tool surface.
create.toolboxesTip: Restrict what the spawned task is allowed to do. The task always keeps a mandatory core set; selected toolboxes add to it. Leave empty to grant the full native tool surface.
create.scheduleInvalid: Invalid cron expression. Use format: minute hour day month weekday
create.oneTimeHelp: Pick a date and time. The job will fire once and then deactivate.
create.datetimePast: Datetime must be in the future

edit.title: Edit scheduled job
edit.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this scheduled job and stop all future executions.

triggerParentTurn.label: Wake the parent Agent at the end of the run
triggerParentTurn.help: The final report of each run triggers an LLM turn on the owner Agent (e.g. to re-read its report and self-calibrate).
triggerParentTurn.warning: This cron will generate an LLM turn on every execution (token consumption).
triggerParentTurn.detail: Each execution wakes the parent Agent for an LLM turn (token consumption).

chat.selectAgent: Select an Agent to start chatting

transfer.outSubtext: This Agent no longer has this channel.
transfer.inSubtext: This Agent now has this channel.

chat.thinkingTokensHint: Output tokens generated so far (updates each step)
chat.thinkingToolsHint: Tool calls this turn - click to view
chat.contextUsage: Context: ~{{tokens}} / {{max}} tokens ({{percent}}%), includes system prompt, history & tools
chat.contextNoData: Context usage will appear after the first message is processed
chat.stepLimitReached: Response truncated: tool step limit reached. Ask me to continue.

emptyTurn.contentFilter: The provider stopped this response before any content was produced (safety filter). Try rephrasing your request.
emptyTurn.length: The model hit its output-token limit before producing any visible content. Try again, or lower the thinking effort.
emptyTurn.generic: The model ended its turn without producing a response (reason: {{reason}}). Try sending your message again.

chat.silentStop: The model ran its tools but produced no final text. Ask it to continue or summarize.

tokenUsage.label: {{total}} tokens
tokenUsage.headlineHint: Real token counts. Click for the full breakdown.

contextSource.estimateHint: Local BPE estimate. The chat banner shows the provider-reported value when available.
contextSource.apiHint: Reported by the provider on the last call (ground truth).
contextSource.realMarkerHint: Real context size reported by the provider on the last call.
contextSource.unattributedHint: Provider-reported size beyond what the local estimator can attribute by section.
contextSource.dualBarHint: The breakdown shows where tokens come from by section, calibrated to match the provider's count. The real bar is what the provider actually counted on the last call.
contextSource.dualBarExplanation: The estimate auto-calibrates against past API roundtrips for this Agent (EMA-smoothed factor), so the two bars normally agree within a few percent. They can still diverge on the very first turn (no observation yet) or right after a major content shift. Give it 2-3 more turns to converge.
contextSource.calibrationHint: EMA-smoothed factor learned from past API roundtrips. Multiplied with the raw BPE count so the estimate tracks what the provider actually charges.

cacheChip.title: Last turn cache
cacheChip.hint: {{hit}}% of input was served from cache. The cache is warm. Your next message should be cheap unless the prefix changes significantly.
cacheChip.hintCold: {{hit}}% of input was served from cache on the last turn, but that was {{ago}} ago, past Anthropic's ~5min ephemeral TTL. The prefix is cold; the next request re-writes it.

contextViewer.title: Context Viewer
contextViewer.structuredHint: Rendered view of the context sent to the LLM. Each section is color-coded to match the context bar. This is a reconstruction for inspection: the actual payload may differ slightly.
contextViewer.rawHint: JSON approximation of the full payload sent to the LLM provider: system prompt, conversation history, and tool schemas. This is a reconstruction for inspection: the actual API call may differ slightly (token masking, message formatting, provider-specific adaptations).

cache.title: Last turn cache
cache.ttlHint: Anthropic ephemeral cache lasts ~5 min from the last turn. After that, the next request pays a full cache write again.

thinkingPicker.title: Reasoning effort
thinkingPicker.unsupported: This model does not support reasoning.

chat.thinkingEnable: Enable extended thinking for this Agent
chat.thinkingDisable: Disable extended thinking for this Agent

welcome.title: Welcome to Gezy
welcome.checklistIntro: Get started in a few simple steps. Gezy will guide you through setting up your AI team.
welcome.allDone: You’re all set! Select an Agent from the sidebar to start chatting.

commands.helpMessage:
/btw - inject context during streaming
/stop - stop generation
/regen - regenerate last response
/compact - compact history
/thinking - toggle extended thinking
/clear - clear conversation

emptyState.defaultRole: Your AI assistant, ready to help.
suggestions: What can you help me with?
emptyState.hint: Type a message below or pick a suggestion to get started

rewind.title: Rewind conversation?
rewind.description_one: This message becomes the most recent one. About {{count}} later message will be permanently deleted from the conversation and its context. This cannot be undone.
rewind.description_other: This message becomes the most recent one. About {{count}} later messages will be permanently deleted from the conversation and its context. This cannot be undone.

toolsBadge.tooltip_one: {{count}} tool available - click to list it
toolsBadge.tooltip_other: {{count}} tools available - click to list them

toolsModal.title: {{name}} - tools
toolsModal.description: Every tool currently exposed to this agent, grouped by domain.
toolsModal.descriptionQuick: Tools exposed in this quick session (session-restricted tools like tasks, crons and inter-agent messaging are excluded).

clear.title: Clear conversation
clear.description: This will permanently delete all messages in this conversation. Memories and files will be preserved. This action cannot be undone.

stats.activityAriaLabel: Message activity sparkline showing conversation frequency over time

quickChat.title: Quick Session
quickChat.empty: Start a quick, private conversation with this Agent. Messages won't appear in the main session.

expired.message: This session has ended or expired.

closing.title: Close quick session?
closing.description: This conversation will not be saved in the main history. You can optionally save a summary as a memory.
closing.summaryPlaceholder: Describe what was discussed or decided...
closing.summaryRequired: Please describe what to remember before saving.

history.title: Session History
history.empty: No past sessions yet. Closed quick sessions will appear here.

taskDetail.runPromptTooltip: Run-specific instructions passed at spawn time
taskDetail.injectPlaceholder: Send a message to the task...

retry.freshTooltip: Spawn a new task with the same description, no message history. The model starts fresh.
retry.forkTooltip: Fork: clone the failed task with all its messages. The model picks up the prior context.

customTools.title: Custom Tools
customTools.description: Global tools you (or your Agents) script in any language. Once created, add them to a toolbox to grant them to an Agent. They run as custom_<slug>.
customTools.emptyDescription: Create a script (Python, Node, Bun, Bash…) that reads JSON args on stdin and writes its result to stdout.
customTools.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete "{{name}}" and its files. Toolboxes that list it will simply skip it.
customTools.installAfterSave: Save the tool first, then install its dependencies.
customTools.testAfterSave: Save the tool first to test it.
customTools.dialogDescription: The script lives on disk in its own directory and may have its own dependencies.

renderer.title: Result renderer (optional)
renderer.help: Optional renderer.tsx that pretty-prints this tool's result in the expanded chat view. Export default function Renderer({ result, args, ui }). Style with the ui kit or inline var(--color-*) tokens (Tailwind classes don't apply); it auto-themes. Leave blank to show the result as JSON.

fields.name: Name
fields.descriptionHint: Tell the LLM what the tool does and when to use it.
fields.parametersHint: JSON Schema object describing the tool's input arguments.

translations.title: Translations
translations.help: UI-only localized labels. They change how the tool is shown in the app (chat, toolbox), never what the LLM sees. Leave blank to use the base values.

errors.name: Name is required
errors.testArgs: Test args must be valid JSON

test.formDisabled: Fix the parameters schema to use the form.
test.invalidJson: Invalid JSON: showing last form values.
test.renderAfterRun: Run a test first to preview the renderer.

help.content: Custom tools let you extend Gezy with your own scripts.
help.bullet1: Each tool is a script in its own folder; it reads arguments as JSON on stdin and prints its result to stdout.
help.bullet2: Add a requirements.txt (Python) or package.json (Node/Bun) and click Install to set up dependencies.
help.bullet3: Grant a tool to an Agent by adding custom_<slug> to one of its toolboxes.

toolDomains.title: Tool Domains
toolDomains.description: Categories used to group and color tools across the UI. Create your own to organize custom tools.
toolDomains.emptyDescription: Create a domain (icon + color + label) to organize your custom tools.
toolDomains.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete the "{{name}}" domain.
toolDomains.dialogDescription: Pick a Lucide icon name and a color token.

fields.label: Label
fields.iconHint: Any Lucide icon name, e.g. CloudSun, Wallet, Bot.

toolboxes.title: Toolboxes
toolboxes.description: Named sets of tools you can assign to tasks. A task's tools are a mandatory core set plus the tools of every toolbox you give it.
toolboxes.emptyDescription: Create a toolbox to group tools and assign them to tasks in one click.
toolboxes.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete the "{{name}}" toolbox. Tasks that referenced it will fall back to the default tools.

form.description: Pick the tools this toolbox grants. Tasks combine the toolboxes you assign with a mandatory core set.
form.viewDescription: This is a built-in toolbox. It cannot be edited or deleted.
form.wildcardNote: This built-in grants every native tool plus every custom tool (MCP and plugin tools must still be added by name).
form.hardExcludedNote: This tool can never run inside a task, even if a toolbox includes it.

help.content: A toolbox is a named, reusable set of native tools. Assign one or more toolboxes to a task and the task can use that union of tools, on top of a mandatory core set that is always available.
help.bullet1: Built-in toolboxes (Code, Research, Ops, Scout, All tools) are read-only but can be assigned to any task.
help.bullet2: Create your own toolboxes to fit recurring kinds of work.
help.bullet3: Every task always keeps a core set of tools, regardless of the toolboxes you assign.
help.bullet4: Some tools can never run inside a task. They are flagged when you build a toolbox.

general.title: General
general.description: Platform-wide settings that apply to all Agents.
general.globalPromptPlaceholder: Enter instructions that all Agents should follow...
general.globalPromptHint: This text is injected into every Agent's system prompt.
general.globalPromptTip: Instructions injected into every Agent's system prompt. Use this for rules or guidelines all your Agents should follow.

publicUrlWarning.title: Public URL may be misconfigured
publicUrlWarning.body: You're accessing Gezy at {{actual}}, but the configured public URL is {{configured}}. Invitation links, webhooks, and OAuth callbacks are built from the public URL, so they'll point to {{configured}} and may be unreachable.
publicUrlWarning.fix: Set PUBLIC_URL to {{actual}} and restart. Docker: -e PUBLIC_URL={{actual}} (or in your .env); native install: GEZY_PUBLIC_URL={{actual}}.

toolsDefaultOpen.label: Expand tool calls by default
toolsDefaultOpen.hint: Show tool call details expanded in every chat (main chat, quick session, tasks). You can still collapse individual calls.

tasks.title: Task execution
tasks.description: Control how many tasks run at once across all Agents. A task only holds a slot while actively executing: suspended tasks (waiting on a human, another Agent, or a sub-task) free their slot.
tasks.hint: Raising the concurrency limit immediately fills free slots from the queue. Lowering it is gradual: running tasks are never stopped, new promotions just pause until the count drops. The queue cap rejects new tasks once that many are already waiting.

maxConcurrent.label: Max concurrent tasks
maxConcurrent.tip: How many tasks may execute simultaneously. When full, new or resuming tasks wait in the queue and start as slots free up. Default 10.

maxQueue.label: Max queued tasks
maxQueue.tip: Anti-runaway guard: how many tasks may wait in the queue before new spawns are rejected. Default 100.

help.content: General settings control platform-wide behavior that applies to all your Agents. The global prompt lets you define instructions every Agent follows.
help.bullet1: The global prompt is injected into every Agent's system prompt. Use it for universal rules or guidelines
help.bullet2: Task execution limits cap concurrent runs across all Agents. Suspended tasks release their slot; raising the limit fills the queue immediately, lowering it is gradual

avatars.title: Avatars
avatars.description: Control the look of generated Agent avatars. These global settings are shared by every newly generated avatar so your Agents look consistent. They don't change existing avatars.
avatars.noProvider: No image provider is configured, so avatars can't be generated yet. Add one to enable avatar generation.

base.title: Base image
base.tip: A neutral reference image used for image-to-image generation, so every avatar shares the same look. Generate one in your chosen style + type, or upload your own. Used only when the image model supports image-to-image.
base.defaultHint: Using the bundled default base image (a friendly robot).
base.enableHint: When on, avatars derive from the base image (image-to-image) for visual consistency. When off, every avatar is generated from text only.

style.title: Art style (A)
style.tip: How avatars are drawn: the rendering style. Pick a preset or type your own. Shared by every generated avatar.
style.hint: A short art-style directive shared by all generated avatars.

subject.title: Subject / type (B)
subject.tip: What every avatar depicts: robot, human, animal, dragon… Pick a preset or type your own. A non-robot subject is generated text-to-image (the bundled base image is a robot).
subject.placeholder: e.g. a small, friendly, cute robot

help.content: Avatars are built from three axes. Two are global (set here); the third is per-Agent.
help.bullet1: Art style (A) and Subject/type (B) are global: every newly generated avatar uses them for a consistent look
help.bullet2: The base image gives image-capable models a reference so avatars share the same render. Generate or upload one, then keep 'Use base image' on
help.bullet3: Each Agent's own character (C) is written automatically from its identity. Tweak a single avatar from the Agent's avatar picker (Manual tab) without touching these globals

bulk.cardTip: Runs the normal auto-generation for each selected Agent using the art direction above. Existing avatars are overwritten.
bulk.cardHint: Apply the current art direction to Agents created before you changed it.
bulk.title: Regenerate avatars
bulk.description: Pick the Agents whose avatars you want to regenerate with the current art direction. Regeneration runs on the server. You can close this window once it starts.

running.note: Regeneration is running on the server. You can safely close this window, it will keep going.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: Manage your provider connections (LLM, embedding, image, search).
providers.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this provider?
providers.emptyDescription: Add an AI provider like OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini to power your Agents.

modelsModal.title: Browse {{name}}
modelsModal.description: Models and voices the provider exposes right now (fetched live from its API). Use this list to verify what is available without leaving Gezy.
modelsModal.emptyAll: This provider exposes nothing to browse.
modelsModal.searchOnlyExplain: Search providers have no model or voice catalogue: they are called directly through the web_search tool. The capabilities above describe what this provider supports.
modelsModal.refreshTooltip: Re-fetch the catalogue from the provider API

help.content: AI providers are the services that power your Agents. Each provider gives access to language models, embedding models, or image generation capabilities.
help.bullet1: Add multiple providers to access different models (GPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.)
help.bullet2: Each Agent can use a different model depending on your needs
help.bullet3: OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, Together, etc.) work with the same configuration pattern
help.bullet4: Test the connection after adding to verify the API key is valid

modelRegistry.title: Model registry
modelRegistry.subtitle: Every model exposed by your providers. Metadata (context, capabilities, pricing) is auto-filled from the community models.dev database. Edit any value to pin it, or remap a wrong match.
modelRegistry.openPageHint: The full table opens on its own page: more room for context, pricing and capabilities.
modelRegistry.displayNameHint: Shown everywhere a model name appears. Leave blank to use the models.dev label (falls back to the id).
modelRegistry.reviewBanner_one: {{count}} model to review: its models.dev match was uncertain. Click ✓ to confirm a match is right, or open a row to remap it.
modelRegistry.reviewBanner_other: {{count}} models to review: their models.dev match was uncertain. Click ✓ to confirm a match is right, or open a row to remap it.
modelRegistry.reviewHint: The models.dev match below was uncertain. Check the values look right (remap if not), then Save to confirm: that clears the review flag.
modelRegistry.confirmTooltip: Confirm this match is correct (clears review)
modelRegistry.resetHint: This model has manual overrides. Reset to drop them and follow models.dev automatically.
modelRegistry.capUnknown: Unknown: neither the provider nor models.dev reported this
modelRegistry.enabledTip: Enabled: shown in model pickers. Click to hide it.
modelRegistry.disabledTip: Disabled: hidden from model pickers. Click to enable.
modelRegistry.enabledDialogHint: Off = hidden from model pickers (the chat path still works if an Agent already uses it).
modelRegistry.snapshotTip: Download the latest models.dev catalogue, then re-match: picks up newly-released models without an app update.
modelRegistry.snapshotRefreshed: models.dev updated: {{models}} models across {{providers}} providers. Re-matching…

models.title: Models & Services
models.description: Configure default models and services used across the platform.
models.defaultLlmTip: Default chat model for new Agents. Each Agent can override it later.
models.defaultLlmHint: Used as the model for every new Agent you create, and by the Agent creation Wizard to generate suggestions from a short description. Without it, you'll have to pick a model manually each time and the Wizard falls back to a fully manual form.
models.defaultCompactingTip: Model that summarizes old conversation history as the context window fills up. A small, fast model is enough.
models.defaultCompactingHint: Used when an Agent's conversation grows beyond its context window: old messages get summarized to free up room. Without it, each Agent uses its own chat model for compacting (works fine but may cost more on long conversations).
models.defaultScoutTip: A cheap, fast model the `scout` tool uses to delegate heavy read-only exploration. A small, fast model is ideal here.
models.defaultScoutHint: Used by the `scout` tool when an Agent or task delegates large read-only exploration (locating code, mapping a subsystem) instead of grinding through it on the expensive model. An Agent or project can override this. Without it, the scout falls back to the calling Agent's own model.
models.defaultScoutPlaceholder: Use the calling Agent's own model (default)
models.defaultImageTip: Image-generation model used by `generate_image` and AI avatar generation.
models.defaultImageHint: Used by the `generate_image` tool and to generate AI avatars when you create a new Agent. Without it, avatars fall back to initials and `generate_image` returns an error telling the Agent no image provider is configured.
models.defaultSearchTip: Search provider used by `web_search` when no specific provider slug is passed.
models.defaultSearchHint: Used by the `web_search` tool. Agents can override per call by passing a `provider_slug`. Without it, `web_search` returns an explicit 'No search provider configured' error to the Agent.
models.defaultTtsTip: Text-to-speech provider used by `text_to_speech` when no specific provider is passed.
models.defaultTtsHint: Used by the `text_to_speech` tool to synthesize audio. The voice is always picked per call (or in a channel's config). Without it, `text_to_speech` returns 'No TTS provider configured': no voice messages, no spoken replies.
models.defaultSttTip: Speech-to-text provider used by `transcribe_audio` when no specific provider is passed.
models.defaultSttHint: Used by the `transcribe_audio` tool to convert audio to text. Without it, `transcribe_audio` returns 'No STT provider configured': no voice transcription, no voicemail-to-text on supported channels.
models.extractionModelTip: Scans past conversations and picks out facts, preferences, and decisions worth saving as long-term memories. A small, fast model works well here.
models.extractionModelHint: Runs automatically during conversation compacting to extract memories. Without it, each Agent uses its own chat model (works fine but can be over-powered and pricier).
models.embeddingModelTip: Converts text into numerical vectors so Agents can search memories by semantic similarity. Changing the model requires re-processing every existing memory.
models.embeddingModelHint: Powers semantic memory search across every Agent's memory tab and the `recall` tool. Without it, memory search falls back to keyword-only (FTS): still works, just less precise at finding loosely related memories.

help.content: Default models are used as fallbacks when creating new Agents or when no specific model is configured at the Agent level.
help.bullet1: The default LLM is pre-selected when creating new Agents, saving time during setup
help.bullet2: Compacting and extraction models can be lightweight: they don't need to be the same as your chat model
help.bullet3: Changing the embedding model requires re-embedding all existing memories

models.defaultScoutThinkingTip: Default reasoning effort for scouts. The chain mirrors the scout model: per-call override, then project, then Agent, then this. Unset = scouts follow the calling Agent's own reasoning config.

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.description: Manage Model Context Protocol servers that provide external tools to your Agents.
mcp.name: Name
mcp.deleteConfirm: This will permanently remove this MCP server and disconnect it from all Agents.
mcp.emptyDescription: MCP servers extend your Agents with external tools and data sources.
mcp.nameTip: A friendly name to identify this server in the list.
mcp.commandTip: The executable to run. Usually npx for npm packages, or an absolute path to a binary.
mcp.argsTip: Command-line arguments passed to the server. Put each argument on its own line.
mcp.envTip: Environment variables passed to the server process. Use these for API keys and secrets.
mcp.envPreserveHint: Leave values empty to keep existing secrets

help.content: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers are external programs that give your Agents additional capabilities beyond their built-in tools. They run as separate processes and communicate via a standard protocol.
help.bullet1: Each MCP server provides one or more tools (e.g. file search, database queries, API integrations)
help.bullet2: Servers are started automatically when an Agent needs them and stopped when idle
help.bullet3: Environment variables let you pass API keys and secrets securely to the server process
help.bullet4: Agents can also discover and add MCP servers on their own (shown as "Pending approval")

vault.title: Vault
vault.description: Manage encrypted entries accessible by all Agents.
vault.descriptionPlaceholder: What is this entry used for?
vault.deleteConfirm: This will permanently remove this entry and its attachments. Agents using it will no longer have access.
vault.allowedToolsTip: Comma-separated tool names allowed to use this secret. Empty: any tool. Example: http_request, run_shell
vault.allowedHostsTip: Comma-separated hosts this secret may be sent to (HTTP and browser tools). Empty: any host. Wildcards like *.example.com match subdomains.
vault.emptyDescription: Store API keys, credentials, cards, notes and files securely.
vault.manageTypesDescription: Create custom entry types with specific field schemas.
vault.typeFormIncomplete: Name, slug, and at least one field with name and label are required
vault.deleteAttachmentConfirm: Permanently delete this attachment? This cannot be undone.
vault.deleteTypeConfirm: Delete this custom type? Existing entries of this type will be kept but no new ones can be created.
vault.keyTip: A unique identifier for this entry (e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN). Agents reference entries by this key.
vault.entryTypeTip: The type determines which fields are available. Choose "Text" for simple values or a structured type for multi-field entries.
vault.descriptionTip: A short note about what this entry is used for. Helps Agents understand when to use it.

help.content: The Vault is a secure key-value store where you can save credentials, API keys, and other secrets. Agents can access vault entries through their tools without the actual secret values appearing in conversations.
help.bullet1: Secrets are encrypted at rest and only decrypted when an Agent needs them
help.bullet2: You can organize secrets by type (API key, password, token, etc.) and create custom types
help.bullet3: Attach files to vault entries for certificates, config files, or other sensitive documents
help.bullet4: Mark entries as favorites for quick access

files.title: Files
files.description: Manage files shared by Agents via shareable URLs.
files.addHint: Upload a file to the shared storage
files.editHint: Update file metadata and access settings
files.name: Display name
files.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this file and invalidate its share URL.
files.emptyDescription: Upload files that your Agents can reference during conversations.

help.content: File Storage lets your Agents save, read, and share files during conversations. Files uploaded by users or generated by Agents are stored here and can be referenced across sessions.
help.bullet1: Each Agent has its own file namespace to avoid conflicts
help.bullet2: Supports images, documents, code files, and any other file type
help.bullet3: Files can be shared in channel messages and webhook payloads

files.agentTip: The Agent that owns this file. Only this Agent can access it unless the file is public.
files.publicTip: Public files get a shareable URL. Private files are only accessible by the owning Agent.
files.passwordTip: Protect the file with a password. Anyone accessing the file will need to enter it.
files.expiresInTip: Auto-delete the file after this many hours. Leave empty to keep it indefinitely.
files.readAndBurnTip: The file is automatically deleted after being downloaded once. Useful for sensitive data.

memories.title: Memories
memories.description: View and manage all Agent memories.
memories.modelConfigRedirect: Memory extraction and embedding models are now configured in Models & Services settings.
memories.modelConfigDescription: Models used for memory extraction and semantic search.
memories.extractionModelHint: Reads your conversations and picks out what's worth remembering. A fast, affordable model works great here.
memories.extractionModelTip: This model scans conversations and decides which facts, preferences, and decisions should be saved as long-term memories. A small, fast model does the job well. If unset, each Agent uses its own chat model.
memories.embeddingModelHint: Turns memories into searchable data so your Agents can find relevant info by meaning, not just keywords.
memories.embeddingModelTip: Converts text into numbers (vectors) so your Agents can search memories by meaning. For example, a memory about "loving pasta" matches a question about "favorite foods". Changing this model requires re-processing all existing memories.
memories.embeddingModelWarning: Warning: changing the embedding model requires regenerating all existing memories.
memories.reembedDescription: Regenerate all memory embeddings using the current embedding model. Use this after changing the embedding model.
memories.reembedConfirm: This will re-embed all memories. It may take a while and will use your embedding API quota. Continue?
memories.reembedFailed: Re-embedding finished with errors: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed out of {{total}}
memories.noEmbeddingHint: Without an embedding provider, memory tools (recall, memorize) fail at call time and search falls back to keyword-only. Configure one in Settings → Models.
memories.subject: Subject
memories.scopeTip: Private memories are only visible to this Agent. Shared memories can be recalled by all Agents.
memories.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this memory. The Agent will no longer recall it.
memories.saveAgentFirst: Save the Agent first to manage its memories.
memories.agentTip: Which Agent this memory belongs to. Each Agent has its own memory space.
memories.contentTip: The information to remember. Be specific, the Agent will recall this verbatim during conversations.
memories.categoryTip: Categorize the memory for better organization and retrieval.
memories.subjectTip: Optional tag to group related memories (e.g. a person's name, a project, a topic).
memories.emptyDescription: Memories help your Agents remember important information across conversations. Add one manually or let your Agents create them automatically.

help.content: Memories let your Agents remember important things from past conversations. Set up the models that handle remembering and searching, and manage all memories here.
help.bullet1: The extraction model reads past conversations and saves the important bits as long-term memories
help.bullet2: The embedding model makes memories searchable by meaning, so your Agent finds relevant info even if the exact words differ
help.bullet3: You don't need expensive models here. Small, fast models handle extraction and embedding just fine
help.bullet4: If no extraction model is set, each Agent uses its own chat model for memory extraction

memories.compactingThresholdDescription: Compact the conversation when context usage reaches this percentage of the model's context window. Lower values compact more often, higher values use more context before compacting.

contacts.title: Contacts
contacts.description: Manage the shared contact registry accessible by all Agents.
contacts.editHint: Update contact information, nicknames and identifiers
contacts.nicknamesTip: Alternative names this contact goes by (online handles, pseudonyms, shortened names). Used for recognition across channels.
contacts.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this contact and all associated nicknames, fields and notes.
contacts.deleteWarnPlatforms: This will also revoke access from {{count}} connected platform(s) ({{platforms}}).
contacts.emptyDescription: Add contacts so your Agents can recognize and personalize interactions.
contacts.revokePlatformIdConfirm: Revoke this platform access? The contact will no longer be able to interact via this platform.
contacts.linkToUserTip: Link this contact to an existing Gezy user account. Lets Agents recognize them across channels.
contacts.identifiersTip: Custom contact fields (email, mobile, Twitter, LinkedIn, Telegram ID, etc.) so Agents can recognize this contact on different platforms.

help.content: The contact registry lets your Agents recognize the people they interact with across different platforms and personalize their responses.
help.bullet1: Link platform identifiers (Telegram, Discord, etc.) so the Agent automatically recognizes users
help.bullet2: Agents can add private notes on each contact to remember context
help.bullet3: Link a contact to a Gezy user account to unify identity across all channels
help.bullet4: Add multiple nicknames so the Agent recognizes a contact by any name they go by

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Manage incoming webhooks that allow external services to notify your Agents.
webhooks.name: Name
webhooks.tokenWarning: Save this token now - it will not be shown again.
webhooks.regenerateConfirm: This will invalidate the current token. External services using it will stop working until updated.
webhooks.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this webhook. External services will receive 404 errors.
webhooks.emptyDescription: Create webhooks to trigger actions or send data to external services.
webhooks.nameTip: A descriptive name for this webhook. Helps you identify it when you have multiple webhooks.
webhooks.descriptionTip: Optional note explaining what this webhook is used for. Visible only to you in the dashboard.
webhooks.filterFieldTip: Dot-notation path to extract a value from the JSON payload.
webhooks.filterAllowedValuesPlaceholder: Type a value and press Enter
webhooks.filterAllowedValuesTip: Only payloads where the extracted field matches one of these values will be forwarded to the Agent.
webhooks.filterAllowedValuesEmpty: No allowed values set: all payloads will be blocked.
webhooks.filterExpressionTip: A regular expression tested against the raw payload body.
webhooks.filterTestPassed: This payload would PASS the filter
webhooks.dispatchModeTip: Conversation mode injects the payload as a message in the Agent's session. Task mode spawns an autonomous sub-task with a configurable prompt template.
webhooks.taskTitleTemplateTip: Template for the spawned task's title. Use {{field.path}} to insert values from the JSON payload. Leave empty for a default title.
webhooks.taskPromptTemplatePlaceholder:
e.g. Analyze this event and take action.

```json
{{__payload__}}
```
webhooks.taskPromptTemplateTip: Template for the task description sent to the Agent. Use {{field.path}} for specific fields or {{__payload__}} for the full raw payload. Leave empty to use the raw payload.
webhooks.maxConcurrentTasksTip: Maximum number of webhook-spawned tasks that can run simultaneously. Additional webhooks are queued and processed when a slot opens. Set to 0 for unlimited.

help.content: Webhooks let external services send events directly to your Agents via HTTP POST requests. Each webhook gets a unique URL and token that you configure in the external service.
help.bullet1: Use webhooks to connect CI/CD pipelines, monitoring alerts, form submissions, or any HTTP-capable service
help.bullet2: Each webhook is linked to a specific Agent that will process the incoming events
help.bullet3: The webhook token must be included as a Bearer token in the Authorization header
help.bullet4: The request body (JSON or text) is forwarded as a message to the target Agent

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage platform users and send invitations.
users.deleteConfirm: This will permanently remove this user's account. They will lose access to the platform.
users.cannotDeleteSelf: You cannot remove your own account

invitations.title: Invitations
invitations.label: Label
invitations.revokeConfirm: This will invalidate the invitation link. Anyone who already has it will not be able to use it.

users.emptyDescription: Invite others to collaborate and use your Agents.

help.content: Manage users who have access to your Gezy instance. Invite friends or colleagues so they can chat with your Agents.
help.bullet1: Create invitation links with a configurable expiration time
help.bullet2: Each user has their own account and conversations
help.bullet3: Invitations can be revoked at any time before they are used

addressBook.title: Address Books
addressBook.subtitle: Connect external address books so your Agents can look up contacts (read-only).
addressBook.addDescription: Connect an external contacts source. Read-only: these never enter Gezy's own contacts.
addressBook.disconnectConfirm: This Agent will no longer be able to read this address book. The contacts themselves are not affected.
addressBook.credentialsLink: Where to generate a {{provider}} app password
addressBook.emptyDescription: Connect iCloud (or another CardDAV source) so your Agents can look up a contact's phone or email.

help.content: Connect an external address book (e.g. iCloud via an app-specific password). Agents can then look up contacts read-only, for example to find a phone number before sending an SMS. These contacts are never copied into Gezy's own contacts.

triggers.empty: No trigger yet for this account.
triggers.dialogDescription: React automatically when a new email matches your conditions.
triggers.name: Name
triggers.conditionsHint: Combine conditions with ALL / ANY groups, nested as needed.
triggers.promptHint: What the agent should do when the trigger fires.
triggers.promptHintTask: Task mode is isolated (no conversation history). Make this self-contained: include all the context the agent needs to act.
triggers.promptPlaceholder: e.g. Summarize this invoice and tell me the amount.
triggers.dispatchHint: Inject into the agent's conversation, or spawn a separate task.
triggers.dispatchConversationDesc: The agent receives the email in its main conversation: it keeps full history and context.
triggers.dispatchTaskWarning: A task runs in an isolated session with NO conversation history. Put everything the agent needs to act in the prompt below.
triggers.oneShotHint: Disables itself after the first matching email.
triggers.deleteConfirm: Delete this trigger? This cannot be undone.
triggers.approvalDescription: When on, triggers an agent sets up stay inactive until you approve them.

emailAccounts.title: Connected Accounts
emailAccounts.description: Connect Google, Microsoft, iCloud or IMAP accounts so your Agents can read/send email and look up contacts. One connection can serve several capabilities.
emailAccounts.addDescription: Choose a provider and connect a mailbox.
emailAccounts.configureAppFirst: Configure the {{provider}} app above before connecting an account.

help.content: Connect a Gmail account via OAuth. First configure a Google Cloud OAuth app (client ID + secret), then click Connect and authorize access.
help.bullet1: Create an OAuth app in Google Cloud Console with the Gmail scopes, and register the redirect URI shown by your Gezy host (…/api/email-accounts/oauth/callback).
help.bullet2: Paste the client ID and secret below, then Connect to authorize the mailbox.
help.bullet3: Grant an Agent the "email" toolbox to let it list, read, search and send mail through connected accounts.

emailAccounts.emptyDescription: Configure a provider above and connect a mailbox to get started.
emailAccounts.alsoContactsHint: Lets Agents look up this account's address book (read-only). Requested in the same consent.
emailAccounts.alsoCalendarHint: Lets Agents read and manage this account's calendar events. Requested in the same consent.
emailAccounts.oauthHelp: OAuth app credentials from your Google Cloud project. The client secret is stored encrypted.
emailAccounts.oauthSetup: Register an OAuth app (type: Web application) in the provider's developer console, grant the mail read + send scopes, and add the redirect URI below.
emailAccounts.redirectUriHelp: Add this exact URI to your OAuth client's "Authorized redirect URIs". Google requires https (or http on localhost).
emailAccounts.secretKeep: Leave blank to keep the current secret
emailAccounts.deleteConfirm: Disconnect this email account? Agents will no longer be able to use it.

channels.title: Channels
channels.description: Connect your Agents to external messaging platforms. Messages will be routed to the selected Agent, and responses sent back automatically.
channels.name: Name
channels.botTokenHint: The token will be encrypted and stored in the vault.
channels.allowedChatIdsHint: Leave empty to accept messages from all chats.
channels.autoCreateContactsHint: Automatically create a contact for unknown senders.
channels.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete the channel and its user mappings. External platform users will no longer be able to reach this Agent.

webhook.label: Inbound webhook URL
webhook.hint: Paste this URL into your provider's console to receive inbound messages. For Twilio: Phone Numbers → your number → Messaging → "A message comes in" → HTTP POST.
webhook.localhostWarning: This points at localhost: external providers can't reach it. Set the PUBLIC_URL env var to your public HTTPS URL so signatures validate and webhooks arrive.

approve.title: Approve user
approve.description: {{name}} from {{platform}} requests access to this channel.

channels.emptyDescription: Set up a channel like Telegram or Discord so your Agents can chat outside the web UI.

setupGuide.title: How to get your bot token

telegram.title: Telegram Setup
steps: Send /newbot and follow the prompts to name your bot
BotFather will give you a token like 123456:ABC-DEF1234ghIkl-zyx57W2v1u123ew11
Copy that token and paste it above
telegram.tip: You can customize your bot later with /setdescription and /setuserpic in BotFather.

discord.title: Discord Setup
steps: Go to discord.com/developers/applications and click "New Application"
Go to the Bot tab and click "Reset Token" to get your token
Enable the Message Content intent under Privileged Gateway Intents
Go to OAuth2 > URL Generator, select "bot" scope with Send Messages permission
Use the generated URL to invite the bot to your server
discord.tip: Make sure the Message Content intent is enabled, or your bot won't receive message text.

channels.nameTip: A friendly name for this channel connection. Helps distinguish multiple channels of the same platform.
channels.agentTip: The Agent that will respond to messages on this channel. Each channel is handled by exactly one Agent.
channels.transferWarning: Selecting a different Agent will transfer this channel. The previous Agent loses the binding and both Agents get an audit-trail row in their conversation.
channels.transferReasonPlaceholder: Optional note about why you're transferring this channel (200 chars max).
channels.transferNoop: Channel is already bound to this Agent.

help.content: Channels connect your Agents to external messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, and more. Each channel routes messages from one platform to a specific Agent.
help.bullet1: Each channel is linked to one Agent that handles all incoming messages
help.bullet2: Test your connection after setup to verify the bot token is valid
help.bullet3: Manage user approvals to control who can chat with your Agent on each platform
help.bullet4: Use allowed chat IDs to restrict which groups or chats the Agent responds to

qr.instructions: Open WhatsApp on your phone, go to Settings > Linked devices > Link a device, and scan this code.
qr.repairHint: The WhatsApp session needs to be linked again. Scan this code to reconnect.

approval.title: Require approval for new contacts
approval.description: New senders must be approved before the Agent replies.
approval.warning: Approval is disabled: anyone who messages this channel is auto-added as a contact and can trigger the Agent (and its costs) without review. A new, distinct contact is created per sender, so never trust a claimed identity. Turn this back on to require manual approval.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Choose which notifications you want to receive.
notifications.noContactsForPlatform: No contacts have a {{platform}} ID. Add a platform ID to a contact first.
notifications.label: Label
notifications.soundDescription: Play a chime when a new notification arrives.
notifications.noAvailableChannels: No active channels available. Create and activate a channel first.
notifications.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this notification channel. You will no longer receive notifications through it.
notifications.sourceChannelTip: The messaging channel (bot) used to send notifications. Must be active and configured.
notifications.recipientTip: The contact who will receive notifications via DM. They must have a platform ID matching the selected channel.
notifications.labelTip: A friendly name to identify this delivery channel in the list.
notifications.noChannelsDescription: Add a delivery channel to receive notifications on Telegram, Discord, email, or other platforms.

help.content: Notifications keep you informed about important events in Gezy. You can toggle individual notification types on or off, and set up external delivery channels to receive them on platforms like Telegram, Discord, or email.
help.bullet1: Toggle notification types to control what you see in the notification bell
help.bullet2: External delivery channels forward notifications to your preferred platforms
help.bullet3: Each delivery channel can filter by notification type for fine-grained control
help.bullet4: Use the test button on delivery channels to verify they work correctly

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Manage plugins that extend Gezy with new tools, hooks, and integrations.
plugins.allUpToDate: All plugins are up to date

empty.title: No plugins installed
empty.description: Drop a plugin folder into the plugins/ directory and click Reload.

plugins.installDescription: Install a plugin from a Git repository or npm package.
plugins.uninstallDescription: Are you sure you want to uninstall "{{name}}"? Plugin files, configured providers (with their API keys), channels and vault secrets will all be removed.

marketplace.title: Browse Plugins
marketplace.description: Plugins published to npm with the `gezy-plugin` keyword. Anyone can publish. Review the package author and source repository before installing.

npmEmpty.title: No results
npmEmpty.description: No package matches your search. Try a broader term.

tokenUsage.title: Token Usage
tokenUsage.description: Monitor LLM token consumption across providers, models, and agents
tokenUsage.noData: No usage data for this period

updates.title: Updates
updates.description: Keep this Gezy instance up to date.
updates.help: Gezy checks GitHub for new versions periodically. The stable channel follows official releases; the edge channel follows the latest commit on the main branch (for testing unreleased changes). Self-update is available for installs managed by install.sh; Docker installs are updated by pulling a newer image.
updates.channelTip: Which stream of updates this instance follows.
updates.channelStableDescription: Follows official releases. Recommended for most installs.
updates.channelEdgeDescription: Follows the latest commit on main. May be unstable. For testing only.

descriptions.prompt-pending: An Agent is waiting for your response to continue
descriptions.channel-user-pending: A new user on a channel needs your approval
descriptions.cron-pending-approval: A scheduled job created by an Agent needs your approval
descriptions.mcp-pending-approval: An MCP server added by an Agent needs your approval
descriptions.agent-error: An Agent encountered an error that may need investigation
descriptions.agent-alert: An Agent sent you a notification
descriptions.mention: Someone mentioned you in a conversation
descriptions.miniapp-notify: A mini-app sent a notification from its background service
descriptions.email-pending-send-approval: An Agent wants to send an email that needs your approval

account.title: My account
account.subtitle: Manage your profile
account.languageHint: Language of menus, buttons, and pages.
account.agentLanguageHint: Language your Agents use when talking to you. Independent from the interface language: Agents can speak languages the interface is not translated into.

password.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters

viewer.title: Tool Calls
viewer.empty: No tool calls yet. Tools will appear here when the Agent uses them.

renderers.fileTruncated: File truncated. Use offset/limit to read more.

invite.title: Join Gezy
invite.subtitle: You've been invited to join this workspace
invite.invalidToken: This invitation link is invalid or has expired.
invite.agentLanguageHint: Language the Agents will use when talking to you.
invite.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

commandPalette.title: Command Palette
commandPalette.description: Search for commands, agents, and settings...

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Press ? anywhere to show this dialog

errorBoundary.message: Something went wrong. Please reload the page.

updateAvailable.title: Update Available
updateAvailable.dockerInstructions: Run the following command in the directory containing your docker-compose.yml:
updateAvailable.dockerTagNote: If your compose file pins a specific version tag, change it to {{version}} (or use the latest tag) before pulling.
updateAvailable.selfUpdateInstructions: Gezy will snapshot its database, download the new version and restart. If anything goes wrong, the previous version is restored automatically.
updateAvailable.devModeNote: This instance is running in development mode. Update it from your git checkout.
updateAvailable.manualInstallNote: This install is not managed by Gezy. Update it the same way it was installed.

miniApps.deleteConfirm: "{{name}}" and all its files will be permanently deleted.

improve.modalDescription: Describe what to add, change or fix. Your request is sent to the maintainer Agent ({{agent}}) so it does the work.
improve.placeholder: e.g. Add a dark mode toggle in the header…

maintainer.label: Maintained by {{agent}}
maintainer.dialogDescription: The maintainer is the Agent responsible for this app and the target of improvement requests. Any Agent can still edit the app.

errors.closeFailed: Failed to close session. Please try again.
errors.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please start a new one.

terminal.title: Terminal
terminal.maxSessions: Session limit reached. Close another terminal session and try again.

disabled.title: Terminal is disabled
disabled.description: This feature is turned off on this instance. Set GEZY_TERMINAL_ENABLED=true and restart the server to enable it.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.closeConfirmDescription: The shell and everything running in it will be killed.
sessions.dormantHint: Click to reconnect: a fresh shell opens in the last directory

tmux.persistentHint: Sessions survive a server restart with their running processes.
tmux.unavailable: tmux not detected: sessions don't survive a restart
tmux.unavailableHint: Install tmux on the host for sessions whose processes survive a restart. Without it, only the scrollback is restored.

presets.title: Session presets
presets.description: Reusable templates: open a session in a directory and run a startup script.
presets.emptyDescription: Create a preset to open sessions in a directory and run a startup command.
presets.cwdHelp: The session starts here. ~ expands to your home. Leave empty for home.
presets.initHelp: Runs once when the session opens, as if typed. One command per line.
presets.deleteConfirmDescription: This preset will be removed. Running sessions are unaffected.

keys.label: Special keys
keys.ctrlHint: Tap Ctrl, then a key (Ctrl+C interrupts)

orphanTask.title: Start a task
orphanTask.description: Launch a standalone task on {{name}}, no project or ticket needed. The result is posted back into this thread when it finishes.
orphanTask.descriptionGeneric: Launch a standalone task, no project or ticket needed. Pick the Agent that should run it; the result is posted back into its thread when it finishes.
orphanTask.agentPlaceholder: Choose an Agent to run this task
orphanTask.promptPlaceholder: e.g. research the top 3 vector databases and summarize trade-offs
orphanTask.promptHelp: Describe the work for this one-off task.
orphanTask.toolboxesHelp: Which tool sets this task can use, on top of the mandatory core set. Leave empty to use the built-in default.
orphanTask.modelHelp: Override the model for this task only. Leave on inherit to use the Agent's own model.
orphanTask.thinkingHelp: Override the reasoning effort for this task only. Leave on inherit to use the Agent's own setting.
orphanTask.started: Task started on {{name}}. The result will appear in this thread when it finishes.

page.title: Projects
page.comingSoon: Kanban view is coming soon: backend is wired up, frontend in progress.

sidebar.title: Projects
sidebar.empty: No projects yet. Create your first project to get started.
sidebar.emptyDescription: Group tickets, run tasks, and track work across your Agents.

empty.title: No projects
empty.description: Create a project from the sidebar to start tracking work.

kanban.searchPlaceholder: Filter tickets by title or tag…

empty.backlog: Ideas land here. Capture them now, plan them later.
empty.todo: Ready to start? Drag tickets here when committed.
empty.blocked: Stuck? Drop blocked tickets here so they stay visible.

quickAdd.label: Add ticket
quickAdd.inputPlaceholder: Ticket title (Enter to add, Esc to cancel)

knowledge.confirmDelete: Delete this knowledge entry? This cannot be undone.

help.title: How project knowledge works
help.pinned: entries have their full content injected into every Agent's prompt automatically (no tool call). Use sparingly (max 10 per project).
help.unpinned: show only their title in the prompt index. Agents fetch the body on demand with get_project_knowledge, or search by topic with search_project_knowledge.
help.scope: All entries are shared across every Agent and ticket task on this project: treat it as a collective wiki.

empty.title: No knowledge yet
empty.description: Capture architectural decisions, conventions, gotchas, and domain rules here. Every Agent working on this project will see the titles in its prompt and can fetch any entry's body on demand.
empty.searchDescription: Try a different query: search uses both semantic similarity and keywords.

dialog.description: Visible to every Agent working on this project. Every entry's title lands in the Agent's prompt; the markdown body is injected only when pinned.
dialog.titlePlaceholder: A short, self-explanatory headline (e.g. 'Use Drizzle ORM, not Prisma')
dialog.titleHint: This is what every Agent will see by default in its prompt. Make it tell the whole story in one line.
dialog.contentPlaceholder:
## Context
Why this matters…

## Details
Code samples, links, reasoning…
dialog.contentHint: Free-form markdown, as long as you need. Only inlined into Agent prompts when pinned; otherwise fetched on demand.
dialog.pinnedOnHint: Pinned: the full markdown content will be injected into every Agent's system prompt for this project. The Agent can use it instantly with no tool call. Cap: 10 pinned entries per project.
dialog.pinnedOffHint: Not pinned: only the title appears in the Agent's prompt. The Agent will fetch the content via the get_project_knowledge tool when the title looks relevant.

create.title: Create a new project
create.description: A default tag palette (bug, feature, chore, doc) will be added automatically. You can customize it later.
create.descriptionPlaceholder: Context, goals, links, constraints: anything useful for an Agent working on this project.
create.descriptionHint: Markdown supported. This description is injected into the system prompt of any Agent that has this project active.

create.title: New ticket
create.description: Add a new ticket to this project. Status defaults to Backlog. Drag it to another column once you're ready to work on it.
create.descriptionPlaceholder: What needs to be done? Any context useful for whoever picks this up.

panel.heading: Ticket
panel.noTasks: No tasks have been spawned on this ticket yet.
panel.noTasksDescription: Spawn an Agent to work on this ticket and track the run here.
panel.notFound: This ticket was not found. It may have been deleted.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete this ticket?
deleteConfirm.description: "{{title}}" will be permanently deleted. Linked tasks history is preserved.

edit.title: Edit ticket
edit.description: Update the title, description, status, or tags. The kanban will refresh in real time.

comments.title: Comments
comments.empty: No comments yet. Be the first to add one.
comments.autoTooltip: Posted automatically from a task result.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete this comment?
deleteConfirm.description: The comment will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.dropHint: Drag and drop files here, or
attachments.noPreview: No preview available. Download the file to open it.
attachments.renameDescription: Choose a new filename. The file content is not modified.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete this attachment?
deleteConfirm.description: "{{name}}" will be permanently removed from disk and from this ticket.

startTask.title: Start a task
startTask.description: Spawn a sub-Agent to work on this ticket. Pre-selected Agent is the one with this project active. The task runs in await mode: you'll get a turn on the Agent's thread when it finishes.
startTask.agentField: Which Agent should run this task?
startTask.runPromptPlaceholder: e.g. focus only on the backend, leave the UI for the next Agent
startTask.runPromptHelp: Optional. Scopes this particular run on top of the ticket. Useful when fanning out several agents in parallel, or stopping at a specific phase. Max 500 chars.
startTask.toolboxesHelp: Which tool sets this task can use. The task always keeps a mandatory core set; selected toolboxes add to it. Defaults to the Code toolbox.
startTask.modelHelp: Override the model for this run only. Leave on inherit to use the project default, then the Agent's own model.
startTask.thinkingHelp: Override the reasoning effort for this run only. Leave on inherit to use the project default, then the Agent's own setting.

enrich.alreadyRunning: An enrichment is already in progress on this ticket.
enrich.title: Enrich this ticket
enrich.description: Spawn a dedicated agent that reads the repo, related tickets, and history, then rewrites the title, description, and tags so the ticket is actionable.
enrich.focusPlaceholder: e.g. dig deeper on the test strategy, or propose a DB migration approach
enrich.focusHelp: Optional. A short orientation passed to the agent.
enrich.started: Enrichment started. The ticket will update live when the agent commits its changes.

chip.tooltip: Active project: {{title}}. Click to view the kanban.

edit.title: Edit project
edit.description: Modify the project details, manage tags, or delete the project entirely.
edit.modelHint: Used by sub-Agent tasks running on tickets of this project: overrides each Agent's own model. A model picked explicitly when spawning a task still wins. Leave empty to fall back to the Agent's model.
edit.scoutModelHint: Cheap, fast model used by the `scout` tool for work in this project: takes precedence over each Agent's scout setting and the global default. Leave empty to fall back to the Agent's scout, then the global default, then the Agent's own model.
edit.thinkingHint: Applied to sub-Agent tasks running on tickets of this project: overrides each Agent's own thinking config. A value chosen explicitly at task spawn still wins.
edit.toolboxesHint: Toolboxes given by default to tasks started on tickets of this project. Toolboxes picked explicitly when starting a task still win. Leave empty to fall back to the built-in default.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete this project?
deleteConfirm.description: "{{title}}" and all its tickets, tags, and ticket history will be permanently deleted. Tasks history is preserved (their ticket link is unset).

edit.scoutThinkingHint: Reasoning effort for scouts dispatched in this project: takes precedence over each Agent's scout reasoning and the global default. Inherit = fall back to the Agent's scout reasoning, then the global default.

tags.empty: No tags yet. Add some below.

github.sectionHint: Pick a GitHub token and the repo to clone. Sub-task worktrees will be created from this clone so multiple tasks can run in parallel without stepping on each other.
github.patPlaceholder: Pick a token from your vault
github.patNoneFound: No credentials in your vault yet. Add one in Settings → Vault (entry type 'credential' or 'text').
github.repoNoneAccessible: No repos accessible with this token. Check the token's scopes.
github.repoNeedsPat: Pick a token first to enable the repo picker.
github.defaultBranchHint: Branch sub-task worktrees are created from. Defaults to the repo's default branch.

statusBlock.cloning: Cloning the repo in the background: sub-tasks will be blocked until this finishes.
statusBlock.ready: Local clone is ready. Sub-tasks can be spawned on this project.
statusBlock.error: Clone failed. Check the message below and click Retry once the issue is fixed.

updateProgress.title: Update in progress
updateProgress.keepOpen: Keep this page open. Gezy is briefly unavailable while it updates and will reload automatically when it's done.
updateProgress.waitingForServer: Server is restarting… waiting for it to come back
updateProgress.failedSafe: Nothing was changed. The current version is still running.
updateProgress.rolledBackDescription: The new version failed to start, so the previous version was restored automatically. Your data was restored from the pre-update snapshot.

addFolder.title: Folders
addFolder.description: Browse and edit an arbitrary folder on the server. Everyone can see and edit folders you add.
addFolder.pathHint: An absolute path to an existing directory on the server.

empty.title: This workspace is empty
empty.description: Files created by this agent will appear here.

noAgents.title: No agents yet
noAgents.description: Create an agent first: its workspace will show up here.

noFileOpen.title: No file open
noFileOpen.description: Select a file in the tree to view it.

tree.loadError: Failed to load - tap to retry
tree.title: Files

deleteConfirm.title: Delete “{{name}}”?
deleteConfirm.fileDescription: This file will be permanently deleted from the workspace.
deleteConfirm.folderDescription: This folder and everything inside it will be permanently deleted.

editor.tooLarge: This text file is too large to edit here ({{size}}). Download it instead.
editor.deletedFromDisk: This file was deleted on disk while you were editing it.

conflict.message: The file changed on disk since you opened it (probably written by the agent).

share.title: Share file
share.snapshotNotice: Creates a frozen copy in the file storage: later changes to the workspace file will not be reflected.

search.placeholder: Search a file by name or path…

profile.firstNameTooLong: First name must be under {{max}} characters
profile.lastNameTooLong: Last name must be under {{max}} characters
profile.pseudonymTooShort: Pseudonym must be at least 2 characters
profile.pseudonymInvalidChars: Pseudonym can only contain letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens

banner.message: Enjoying Gezy? A quick star or a word of feedback helps a lot.

dialog.title: Share your feedback
dialog.description: Bug, idea, or just your experience: anything helps improve Gezy.

messagePlaceholder.bug: Describe what went wrong and how to reproduce it...
messagePlaceholder.experience: Tell us how it is going...

dialog.emailHint: Leave it if you would like a reply.
